title: ericoides praestans
intro: Ericoides praestans, also known as Mountain Club Moss, is a low-growing, evergreen plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal and Maori medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting respiratory health. Ericoides praestans can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
